title: Sudarśana Chakra Sādhana
century: Ahirbudhnya Saṃhitā c. 200–450 CE; Sudarśana Kavaca later medieval
description: A Pāñcarātra-Āgamic Śrī Vaiṣṇava rite addressed to Sudarśana — the discus of Viṣṇu, personified as the *āyudhapuruṣa* ("weapon-person") Chakraratin — primarily as a protective (*rakṣā*) and *abhicāra*-counter practice, often paired with Nārasiṃha upāsanā. Moderate caution — a Pāñcarātra practice historically transmitted under Ācārya supervision but more broadly accessible than Mahāvidyā rites. Pre
